Check-in & Check-out
Check-in
From 4:00 PM
We will be in touch before your arrival to let you know exactly how to access the property.
Check-out
By 10:00 AM
Leaving early? Leave keys inside and drop the entrance key in the lockbox. Ask us for the code during your stay.
Keys
🔑
With you
2 × main entrance · 2 × kitchen door · 1 × kitchen shutter
🏠
Stay inside the property
1 × living room french doors (leave on the door) · 1 × gate fob (drawer in entrance hall) · 1 × jacuzzi key (buoy hooks in kitchen)
The large bay window in the living room has no key — it locks from the inside only.
Cleaning on departure
Remove bedsheets from all beds that were used
Place used towels in the bathrooms
Make sure bins are emptied and rubbish is sorted correctly before you leave
Emergencies
⚡ Power cut? Check the trip switches in the fuse box in the cupboard under the porch, to the left of the front door. Do not touch the EDF box at the roadside.
Emergency trades
🔧 Plumber — Lemazurier Navarre: +33 2 33 46 58 07
⚡ Electrician — Guéret Elec: +33 6 62 73 19 00
Living Room
Two large sofas, pouffes and an armchair. Freebox TV — connect your own streaming apps but please log out before you leave. There is also a digital piano. Board games, books and toys are in the storage pouffes.
Heating
The wood-pellet burner in the living room is the primary — and most efficient — heat source for the whole property. It also creates a wonderfully cosy atmosphere.
Wood-pellet burner
The burner is straightforward to use. Pellets are stored in the bag to the right of the burner. Fill the hopper at the top, select your desired heat level on the control panel, and press the power button. It will self-ignite within a few minutes. To turn off, press the power button — it will run a cool-down cycle before stopping fully. Please do not leave the burner unattended when the pellet level is very low.
📄

How to use the pellet burner
Video coming soon
How to clean the pellet burner
Tap to watch
Kitchen & Dining
Fully equipped: dishwasher, fridge/freezer, induction hob, oven, microwave, blender and electric hand mixer (for whipping cream etc.). A second fridge is available in the room off the living room for larger groups. The dining table seats 12 comfortably. Please ensure you use a trivet to protect the table from hot dishes. The raclette machine for 12 (with fondue, crêpe & cooking stone options) can be found in the ottoman of the large sofa. Everything you need to open crabs and seafood is in the kitchen.
💡 Some kitchen cupboards push open rather than pull!
Appliances
Operating manuals for all appliances can be found in the laundry room.
Dishwasher
Put a tablet in the door compartment · Press on · Press P to choose: (3) Eco 197min · (4) Quick 60min · (5) Intensive 143min · Press Start and close door
Induction hob
Hold ⏼ for 1 second to start · Select zone · Use +/− for heat · Zones 8 & 9 are very powerful, use with care · Hold ⏼ to turn off
📖 Full manual
Oven
Turn the right-hand dial to the fan oven symbol · Set temperature with + and − on the centre screen
📖 Full manual
Washing machine
Liquid detergent → left compartment · Softener → middle · Powder/tablets → directly in drum · Select programme and press DÉPART/PAUSE
Dryer
No need to empty water — automatic drainage. Clean the lint filter after every use. Most eco cycle: SÉCHAGE COTON. Two washing lines in the garden + pegs in laundry room.
📖 Full manual
Bedrooms & Towels
One bath towel and one hand towel are provided per guest, for indoor use only. Please do not take them to the beach.
The Rooms

BEDROOM 3 · Double

Bathrooms
🚿
Bathroom 1 · Ground floor
Large shower · toilet · power socket
🛁
Bathroom 2 · 1st floor
Shower over bath · toilet
✨
En-suite · Bedroom 1
Large shower · toilet · power socket
🌊
Spa wet room
Shower + toilet for spa guests
🏖️ Please rinse sand off outside before coming in — hosepipe at the back of the house. Sand blocks the drains.
Electric heaters
Each bedroom and bathroom has an electric radiator. Please never leave windows open while heaters are on, and always turn radiators off when leaving the property. Please be mindful of energy use.
🌿 The pellet burner is the most eco-friendly option. Electric heaters are a backup — please use them sparingly.
Spa & Garden
The spa is accessed via the wooden walkway to the rear. The key hangs in the kitchen above the island countertop. Please keep it inside the property and lock the spa when not in use — especially important for the safety of children.
How to use the jacuzzi
Tap to watch
Troubleshooting the jacuzzi
Tap to watch
Jacuzzi rules
Maximum 6 people
Not intended children
🚿 Please shower thoroughly before entering — sand gets into the filter and can break the jacuzzi
Never use after drinking alcohol
No smoking inside the spa
Replace the cover after use to maintain water temperature
Use the squeegee to push excess water toward the drain
Garden & BBQ
We have a lovely stone BBQ in the garden. Please only use charcoal — this is important to protect the BBQ. Tools are provided. Extendable outdoor table seating 8–12, with 12 garden chairs.
⛱️ Please always fold the parasol away when not in use and at night — it can cause damage in high winds. Please also secure the garden chairs and shutters in case of strong winds.
House Rules
Recycling & Rubbish
🗑
Grey transparent bags
Non-recyclable waste: food scraps, sanitary products, tissues
♻
Yellow bags
Recyclables: paper, cardboard, plastic and metal packaging
🍾
Glass
Drop at the glass bank at 1 Rte de la Mer (2 min by car)
📅 Collection days 2026: every Monday all year · also every Friday from 17 Jun to 15 Sep.

Put bags out at the corner of the property after 6PM the evening before collection.

⚠️ Putting the wrong things in the wrong bags means the rubbish will not be collected, and you will be charged.
📄 Official COCM recycling guide (PDF)
Smoking
No smoking inside the property or spa at any time. You are welcome to smoke in the garden — please use the ashtrays provided.
Pets
Welcome with a €30 supplement per stay. Pets must not sleep in the beds. Nearest vet: Cabinet des Polders, Port-Bail-sur-Mer — 15 min by car.
Toilets
Toilet paper only — no sanitary products, wipes or anything else.
Security & Wind
Lock all windows and doors before leaving. In high winds, please secure the shutters flat against the wall, fold away the parasol and bring in the garden chairs.
Parking
Free parking along Rue du Sémaphore and Rue des Bleuets. You can also park inside the property through the rolling gate.
Sustainability
We recognise that as holiday rental owners we have a responsibility to promote sustainable and responsible tourism. We have committed to: promoting efficient use of water and energy, providing biodegradable cleaning products, providing recycling bins, and working towards recognised environmental certification. We encourage you to think green during your stay too — small things like shorter showers, turning off lights, and recycling correctly make a real difference. 🌿
Restaurants (4 min walk)
♥Le Grain de Sable ↗
Seafood restaurant with a large covered terrace. Mussels & chips, grilled meats, fish of the day. Live music all summer. Highly recommended.
€€SeafoodLive music
La Bohème ↗
Family restaurant with covered terrace and beachy vibe. Daily chalkboard menu, tapas, galettes. Closed in winter.
€€
Le Vent des Isles ↗
Bar-snack at great prices. Karaoke every Friday.
€Bar
O La La Bar ↗
A newer bar in the village, apparently very good — worth checking out!
Bar
Food Shopping
♥Proxi — 4 min walk ↗
Open daily except Wednesday, 8AM–8PM. Fresh bread & croissants every morning.
♥Seafood vending machines — 5 min ↗
Main village car park · Open 24/7. Fresh oysters & seafood from Fermes Marines du Noroit.
♥Maison Viard (bakery) — 12 min by car ↗
Best bakery in the area, highly recommended. Summer kiosk also on Route de la Mer (4 min walk).
♥Boucherie F. Martinel — 14 min ↗
17 Rue du Dr Callegari, La Haye. One of the best butchers in the area.
Intermarché Lessay — 13 min ↗
Mon–Sat 8AM–7:30PM · Sun 9:30AM–12:30PM. Click & collect available · 24/7 petrol station.
